diagnosis: Layer 3 code bug: `train.py` assumes a newer PyTorch AMP API (`torch.amp.GradScaler`) that is not available in the current runtime (`torch==2.2.1+cu121`). This causes an immediate `AttributeError` before training starts. The environment/GPU setup is healthy (`cuda_avail=True`, correct CUDA build), so this is not infrastructure. Root cause is version-incompatible AMP API usage in code.

patches.old:
use_amp = bool(args.fp16 and device.type == "cuda")
    scaler = torch.amp.GradScaler("cuda", enabled=use_amp)

    lambda_consistency, lambda_contrastive = get_mode_weights(args)
patches.description: Adds PyTorch-version-compatible AMP setup by falling back to `torch.cuda.amp.GradScaler` when `torch.amp.GradScaler` is unavailable, and prepares a compatible autocast context helper.
Uses the compatibility wrapper for autocast so AMP works across PyTorch versions without crashing.
